---
title: "Rib Curvature and Haemal Spine Angle"
author: "Callum Bucklow"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r Libraries}
require(dplyr) #for manipulation of dataframes
require(stringr) #for useful string codes
require(geomorph) #for geometric morphometric analysis
require(ape)
require(ggplot2)
require(caper)
require(Morpho)
require(tidyverse)
require(mvMORPH)
require(phytools)
require(viridis)
require(rgl) #for 3D visualisation
require(pracma) #for cross-product calculation (in calculation of kappa)
```

# Rib Curvature

```{r Import Data}

precaudal <- readland.tps("Data/precaudal.tps",
                              specID = c("ID"), readcurves = TRUE)

#drop Chindongo elongatus (broken neural spines)
precaudal <- precaudal[, , !grepl("Chindongo_elongatus_", dimnames(precaudal)[[3]]), drop = FALSE]

#drop hybrids
precaudal <- precaudal[, , !grepl("M_zebra_", dimnames(precaudal)[[3]]), drop = FALSE]

#import species data for specimens
species_data <- read.csv('Data/species_data.csv')

#remove 'ruaha blue' from species_data
species_data <- 
  species_data %>%
  filter(!species_name %in% c("Astatotilapia sp. 'Ruaha blue'", "Chindongo elongatus")) #ribs are fine for Chindongo elongatus, but it was filtered out of PCA analysis, for consistency remove it here too. 

#import the tree
tree <- read.tree(file='Trees/phylogenies_mcgee_2020/phylogeny_mcgee_2020.tre')

#remove _ from tip labels
tree$tip.label <- gsub("_", " ", tree$tip.label)

#prune tree
tips_to_keep <- species_data$species_name

#prune tree to tips present within data:
pruned_tree <- keep.tip(tree, intersect(tree$tip.label, tips_to_keep))

#reorder dataframe to match order of tips
species_data <- species_data[match(pruned_tree$tip.label, species_data$species_name), ]

#remove the damn X column
species_data <- species_data %>% 
  dplyr::select(-X)
```

```{r Extract Coordinates for Each Pleural Rib}

#we want to extract the raw coordinates for each vertebrae
rib_curvature_coordinates <- precaudal[16:27, , ]

#reposition landmark 17 (now landmark 2) to the end of the array to match that it's marking the ventral tip of the pleural rib
rib_curvature_coordinates_reordered <- rib_curvature_coordinates[c(1, 3:12, 2), , ]

#this should pop-up a 3D plot of all of the aligned coordinates in X,Y,Z space
plotAllSpecimens(rib_curvature_coordinates_reordered[,,1:3], #note that they are already arranged alphabetically (and therefore by species, so the first three are precaudal vertebrae belonging to the same species so will be on the same scale)
                 label = TRUE, 
                 plot.param = list(txt.cex = 2.00, 
                                   txt.adj = 5.00, 
                                   txt.col = 'red'))
```

```{r Procrustes Superimposition on Precaudal Vertebrae (ProcD)}
#define the sliding landmarks (i.e. for rib-curvature)
#define sliding landmarks (18 to 27) with fixed landmarks at 16 and 17 -- remember we have redefined 27 (in the original landmark scheme as the tip of the rib as landmark 17 as it is fixed)
num_sliders <- 10  # Since sliding landmarks are 18 to 27
precaudal_sliders <- matrix(NA, nrow = num_sliders, ncol = 3)

for (i in 1:num_sliders) {
  precaudal_sliders[i, ] <- c(17 + i, 17 + i - 1, 17 + i + 1)
}

#manually adjust first and last row of the matrix:
precaudal_sliders[1, ] <- c(18, 16, 19)
precaudal_sliders[10, ] <- c(27, 26, 17)

#general procrustes superimposition on all caudal coordinates
gpa_precaudal_procd <- gpagen(precaudal, curves = precaudal_sliders, max.iter = 100, ProcD = FALSE) #FALSE here minimises bending energy

#extract specimen IDs
specimen_ids <- dimnames(gpa_precaudal_procd$coords)[[3]]  #extract specimen names
species <- gsub("(^[A-Za-z]+_[a-z]+).*", "\\1", specimen_ids)  #extract Genus_species

#filter for unique species
unique_species <- unique(species)

# Extract centroid sizes for all specimens
centroid_sizes <- gpa_precaudal_procd$Csize

# Initialize vector to store mean centroid size per species
species_centroid_means <- numeric(length(unique_species))
names(species_centroid_means) <- unique_species

# Loop through species and compute mean centroid size
for (i in seq_along(unique_species)) {
  species_centroid_means[i] <- mean(centroid_sizes[species == unique_species[i]])
}

#convert to dataframe
centroid_size_df <- data.frame(
  species_name = gsub("_", " ", names(species_centroid_means)),
  centroid_size = as.numeric(species_centroid_means)
)

#append to species_data for downstream analysis
species_data <-
  species_data %>%
  left_join(centroid_size_df, by='species_name')
```

```{r Fit Splines to All Ribs and Plot them All (Messy)}
#open a 3D plot window
open3d()

#loop over the first three specimens
for (i in 1:171) {
  coords <- rib_curvature_coordinates_reordered[,,i]
  
  #parameterize by landmark index
  t <- 1:nrow(coords)
  
  #fit spline for each coordinate axis
  fx <- splinefun(t, coords[,1], method = "natural")
  fy <- splinefun(t, coords[,2], method = "natural")
  fz <- splinefun(t, coords[,3], method = "natural")
  
  #evaluate spline at 25 evenly spaced points
  t_interp <- seq(1, nrow(coords), length.out = 25)
  x_spline <- fx(t_interp)
  y_spline <- fy(t_interp)
  z_spline <- fz(t_interp)
  
  #plot original landmarks
  points3d(coords, col = 'red', size = 5)
  
  #plot spline curve
  lines3d(x_spline, y_spline, z_spline, col = 'blue', lwd = 2)
}

#add axis labels
axes3d()
title3d(xlab = "X", ylab = "Y", zlab = "Z")
```

```{r Define a Function to Calculate Kappa curvature at each point (n=25)}

compute_spline_curvature_by_index <- function(coords, specimen_id = NA, n_points = 25) {
  t <- 1:nrow(coords)
  
  #fit 1d natural cubic splines for x, y, and z coordinates
  fx <- splinefun(t, coords[,1], method = "natural")  #using a natural cubic spline fitting
  fy <- splinefun(t, coords[,2], method = "natural")
  fz <- splinefun(t, coords[,3], method = "natural")
  
  #interpolate spline fits at evenly spaced points
  t_interp <- seq(1, nrow(coords), length.out = n_points)
  x_spline <- fx(t_interp)
  y_spline <- fy(t_interp)
  z_spline <- fz(t_interp)
  
  #compute first derivatives (tangent vectors)
  dx1 <- fx(t_interp, deriv = 1)
  dy1 <- fy(t_interp, deriv = 1)
  dz1 <- fz(t_interp, deriv = 1)
  
  #compute second derivatives (change in tangent vectors)
  dx2 <- fx(t_interp, deriv = 2)
  dy2 <- fy(t_interp, deriv = 2)
  dz2 <- fz(t_interp, deriv = 2)
  
  #calculate curvature (kappa) at each interpolated point
  #using the formula: ||v' × v''|| / ||v'||^3
  curvature <- sapply(1:n_points, function(i) {
    v1 <- c(dx1[i], dy1[i], dz1[i])   #first derivative vector
    v2 <- c(dx2[i], dy2[i], dz2[i])   #second derivative vector
    cross_prod <- pracma::cross(v1, v2)  #cross product of v' and v''
    num <- sqrt(sum(cross_prod^2))       #norm of cross product (numerator)
    denom <- sqrt(sum(v1^2))^3           #cube of norm of first derivative (denominator)
    if (denom == 0) return(NA)           #avoid division by zero
    num / denom
  })
  
  list(
    specimen_id = specimen_id, #to assign each entry with it's respective specimen ID, note has to be specified before running the function
    spline_points = cbind(x_spline, y_spline, z_spline),
    curvature = curvature,
    t_interp = t_interp
  )
}

```

```{r Plot Kappa Profiles for All Vertebrae}

#define specimen IDs
specimen_ids <- dimnames(rib_curvature_coordinates_reordered)[[3]]

#initialize empty lists for results and outliers
kappa_results_list <- list()
outliers_list <- list()
curvature_threshold <- 2

#loop once over all specimens
for (i in seq_along(specimen_ids)) {
  coords <- rib_curvature_coordinates_reordered[,,i]
  result <- compute_spline_curvature_by_index(coords)
  
  #assign specimen ID inside the result list
  result$specimen_id <- specimen_ids[i]
  
  max_curv <- max(result$curvature, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  if (max_curv > curvature_threshold) {
    outliers_list[[length(outliers_list) + 1]] <- list(index = i, result = result, max_curvature = max_curv)
  } else {
    kappa_results_list[[length(kappa_results_list) + 1]] <- result
  }
}

#plot filtered ribs only
plot(NULL, 
     xlim = c(1, 12), 
     ylim = c(0, max(sapply(kappa_results_list, function(x) max(x$curvature, na.rm = TRUE)))), 
     xlab = "Landmark Index", 
     ylab = "Curvature (Kappa)",
     main = "All Precaudal Vertebrae")

alpha_black <- adjustcolor("black", alpha.f = 0.1)

for (i in seq_along(kappa_results_list)) {
  lines(kappa_results_list[[i]]$t_interp, kappa_results_list[[i]]$curvature, col = alpha_black, lwd = 1)
}

#create a dataframe of outliers or an empty dataframe if none
if (length(outliers_list) > 0) {
  outlier_indices <- sapply(outliers_list, function(x) x$index)
  outlier_df <- data.frame(
    index = outlier_indices,
    max_curvature = sapply(outliers_list, function(x) x$max_curvature),
    specimen_id = specimen_ids[outlier_indices],
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
} else {
  outlier_df <- data.frame(index = integer(0), max_curvature = numeric(0), specimen_id = character(0))
}

#output dataframe of outliers
outlier_df
```

```{r Quantify Global Curvature for Each Rib}

#we can quantify the global curvature by taking and solving the integral for each of spline functions
#i.e.: ∫ κ(t) dt over the length of the curve, where κ(t) is the curvature function, and t parametrises the curve (the interpolated landmark index).

#define function to integrate according to the trapezoid rule:
compute_total_curvature <- function(curvature_values, t_interp) {
  #remove any NA values
  valid_idx <- !is.na(curvature_values)
  #numerical integration using trapezoidal rule
  pracma::trapz(t_interp[valid_idx], curvature_values[valid_idx])
}

#calculate global curvature for each rib
total_curvature_list <- numeric(length(kappa_results_list))

for (i in seq_along(kappa_results_list)) {
  total_curvature_list[i] <- compute_total_curvature(kappa_results_list[[i]]$curvature, 
                                                    kappa_results_list[[i]]$t_interp)
}

#create total curvature dataframe
#get the specimen iDs
specimen_ids_filtered <- sapply(kappa_results_list, function(x) x$specimen_id)

#assemble dataframe
total_curvature_df <- data.frame(
  specimen_id = specimen_ids_filtered,
  total_curvature = total_curvature_list,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

#print the dataframe
#total_curvature_df

#wrangling of dataframe to get species
total_curvature_df$species_name <- sub("^([A-Za-z]+_[a-z]+).*", "\\1", total_curvature_df$specimen_id) #extract Genus_species

#replace underscore with space
total_curvature_df$species_name <- gsub("_", " ", total_curvature_df$species_name)

#calculate species means
total_curvature_means <- 
  total_curvature_df %>%
  group_by(species_name) %>%
  summarise(mean_global_curvature = mean(total_curvature),
            ln_mean_global_curvature = mean(log(total_curvature))) %>%
  filter(species_name != 'Astatotilapia sp') #remove ruaha blue


total_curvature_means
#convert to dataframe
total_curvature_means <- as.data.frame(total_curvature_means)

#associate with the rest of the species data
species_data <- 
  species_data %>%
  left_join(total_curvature_means, by='species_name')

species_data <- 
  species_data %>%
  mutate(normalised_mean_curvature = mean_global_curvature / centroid_size,
         ln_normalised_mean_curvature = log(normalised_mean_curvature))
```

```{r PGLS Model (Rib Curvature ~ Body Elongation)}

#fit OLS model for comparison
rib_curvature_elongation_ols <- lm(ln_normalised_mean_curvature ~ ln_length_ln_width, data=species_data)
summary(rib_curvature_elongation_ols)

#fit PGLS model
#create a comparative data object in caper (i.e. vcv matrix)
comp_data <- comparative.data(pruned_tree, species_data, species_name, vcv=TRUE)

#fit the PGLS model and estimate lambda via ML
rib_curvature_elongation_pgls <- pgls(ln_normalised_mean_curvature ~ ln_length_ln_width, comp_data, lambda="ML")

#summarise model fit
summary(rib_curvature_elongation_pgls)
```

```{r Plot Rib Curvature against Body Elongation}

ggplot(species_data, aes(x=ln_length_ln_width, y=ln_normalised_mean_curvature))+
  geom_point(aes(fill = lm_ecology), shape = 21, size = 4, color = "black")+
  geom_text(aes(label = species_name), size = 1.5, vjust = -0.5)+ #add species names
  geom_abline(intercept = rib_curvature_elongation_ols$coefficients[[1]], 
              slope = rib_curvature_elongation_ols$coefficients[[2]], 
              size = 1, colour = 'black', linetype = 'solid')+ #ols
   geom_abline(intercept = rib_curvature_elongation_pgls$model$coef[[1]], 
              slope = rib_curvature_elongation_pgls$model$coef[[2]], 
              size = 1, colour = 'red', linetype = 'dashed')+ #pgls 
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(
      "Astatotilapia" = "#E87D72",
      "Rhamphochromis" = "#51B3E6",
      "Diplotaxodon" = "grey",
      "Deep Benthic" = "#BD9C33",
      "Shallow Benthic" = "#A18BF8",
      "Utaka" = "#FC61D7",
      "Mbuna" = "#00C095"
    ))+
  theme_classic()+
    theme(legend.position = "none",
          axis.text = element_text(size = 14),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
          axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, vjust=0, hjust=0.5, size=14))
```

# Haemal Spine

```{r Import Caudal Vertebral Data}

caudal <- readland.tps("Data/caudal.tps",
                              specID = c("ID"), readcurves = FALSE)

#drop Chindongo elongatus (broken neural spines)
caudal <- caudal[, , !grepl("Chindongo_elongatus_", dimnames(caudal)[[3]]), drop = FALSE]

#drop hybrids
caudal <- caudal[, , !grepl("M_zebra_", dimnames(caudal)[[3]]), drop = FALSE]
```

```{r Procrustes Superimposition on Caudal Vertebrae}

#general procrustes superimposition on all caudal coordinates
gpa_caudal <- gpagen(caudal, max.iter = 100)

#extract specimen IDs
specimen_ids <- dimnames(gpa_caudal$coords)[[3]]  #extract specimen names
species <- gsub("(^[A-Za-z]+_[a-z]+).*", "\\1", specimen_ids)  #extract Genus_species

#filter for unique species
unique_species <- unique(species)

#create an empty array to store species mean shapes
species_means <- array(NA, dim = c(dim(gpa_caudal$coords)[1], dim(gpa_caudal$coords)[2], length(unique_species)))

#loop through species and compute mean shape
for (i in seq_along(unique_species)) {
  species_means[,,i] <- mshape(gpa_caudal$coords[,,species == unique_species[i]]) #mshape is a function in geomorph that estimates mean shape for a set of ALIGNED specimens.
}

#assign species names to the array
dimnames(species_means)[[3]] <- unique_species

#extract specimen IDs
specimen_ids <- dimnames(gpa_caudal$coords)[[3]]  #extract specimen names
species <- gsub("(^[A-Za-z]+_[a-z]+).*", "\\1", specimen_ids)  #extract Genus_species

#filter for unique species
unique_species <- unique(species)

#create an empty array to store species mean shapes
species_means <- array(NA, dim = c(dim(gpa_caudal$coords)[1], dim(gpa_caudal$coords)[2], length(unique_species)))

#loop through species and compute mean shape
for (i in seq_along(unique_species)) {
  species_means[,,i] <- mshape(gpa_caudal$coords[,,species == unique_species[i]]) #mshape is a function in geomorph that estimates mean shape for a set of ALIGNED specimens.
}

#assign species names to the array
dimnames(species_means)[[3]] <- unique_species

#replace underscore _ with ' ' in species_names
dimnames(species_means)[[3]] <- gsub("_", " ", dimnames(species_means)[[3]])
```

```{r Define Function to Calculate Hameal Spine Angle (Relative to Centrum)}

#using the cosine rule

#cos^-1(B) = (a^2+c^2-b^2)/2ac, where a, b and c are the euclidean distances of defined landmarks
haemal_arch_angle <- function(coords) {
  a <- sqrt(sum((coords[4, ] - coords[13, ])^2))
  b <- sqrt(sum((coords[4, ] - coords[17, ])^2))
  c <- sqrt(sum((coords[13, ] - coords[17, ])^2))
  
  #cosine rule denominator
  denom <- 2 * a * c
  
  #avoid division by zero or invalid values for acos()
  if (denom == 0) {
    return(NA)
  }
  
  #calculate cos(C)
  cos_angle <- (a^2 + c^2 - b^2) / denom
  
  #clamp the value between -1 and 1 to prevent domain error
  cos_angle <- max(min(cos_angle, 1), -1)
  
  #calculate the angle in radians
  angle_rad <- acos(cos_angle)
  
  #convert to degrees (note none are 0 or >2pi)
  angle_deg <- angle_rad * (180 / pi)
  
  return(angle_deg)
}
```

```{r Calculate Mean Haemal Arch Angle (Relative to Centrum)}

#calculate the angle of the tip of the neural arch relative to the centrum for all species
haemal_arch_angles <- apply(species_means, 3, haemal_arch_angle)

#convert into a dataframe for easier manipulation
haemal_arch_angles_df <- data.frame(
  species_name = names(haemal_arch_angles),
  haemal_arch_angle = haemal_arch_angles
)

#join the angles to the species data
species_data <- 
  left_join(species_data, haemal_arch_angles_df, by='species_name')
```

```{r Fit OLS and PGLS Models to Haemal Spine Angle}
#fit OLS model
haemal_spine_angle_elongation_ols <- lm(haemal_arch_angle ~ ln_length_ln_width, data=species_data)
summary(haemal_spine_angle_elongation_ols)

#fit PGLS model
#create a comparative data object in caper (i.e. vcv matrix)
comp_data <- comparative.data(pruned_tree, species_data, species_name, vcv=TRUE)

#fit the PGLS model and estimate lambda via ML
haemal_spine_angle_elongation_pgls <- pgls(haemal_arch_angle ~ ln_length_ln_width, comp_data, lambda="ML")

#summarise model fit
summary(haemal_spine_angle_elongation_pgls) 
```
